zh.Prescot, Lancashire, England: 英國藍卡爾普雷斯可特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Bold於1160年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾波爾德時，他的父親William de Bold32歲，母親Alicia35歲。他於1189年與Waltinia在英國藍卡爾波爾德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1201年在英國藍卡爾波爾德享年41歲。

zh-hans.Prescot, Lancashire, England: 英国藍卡爾普雷斯可特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert de Bold于1160年出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾波爾德时，他的父亲William de Bold32岁，母亲Alicia35岁。他于1189年与Waltinia在英国藍卡爾波爾德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1201年在英国藍卡爾波爾德享年41岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Staffordshire): from Middle English bold ‘dwelling-house’ (Old English bold the West Midland and northwestern form of Old English bōthl, bōtl), especially in the sense ‘castle or manor house’ for someone who lived or worked at the main house, or a habitational name from one of the many places called with this word, for example Bold (Lancashire), The Bold in Aston Botterell (Shropshire), Booden Farm in Haughton, Booth in Blithfield, and Bull Bridge in Penkridge (all Staffordshire). Compare Bodle .
English: nickname from Middle English bold, Old English b(e)ald ‘bold, brave, noble’, which in Middle English developed the additional sense ‘arrogant, impudent, shameless’.
English: variant of   Bolt   and possibly a variant of   Bald   2.

variants.name: Bollmann
meanings: German:
elaborated derivative of the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo (see Bold 4).

topographic name for a man who lived on a rounded hill, from Middle Low German bol ‘rounded hill’ + man ‘man’, or a habitational name for someone from any of several places called Boll (see Boll ). Compare Bolman .

variants.name: Bowlds
meanings: English: possibly a variant of   Bold  .

variants.name: Boldman
meanings: Probably an Americanized form of German Boldemann: from a compound of a variant of the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo (see Bold 4) + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Bodle
meanings: English: habitational name from Buddle Oak in Halse (Somerset), Buddle in Fordingbridge (Hampshire), Buddle in Niton (Isle of Wight), Budleigh in Moretonhampstead (Devon), or Budle in Bamburgh (Northumberland), all named from Middle English bothel, Old English bothl ‘dwelling, house’, probably denoting a homestead of some considerable size. See also Bold .
English (West Midlands): variant of   Beadle  .

variants.name: Bolds
meanings: English: variant of Bold , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Boldt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hermann, Kurt, Bernhard, Dieter, Egon, Gerd, Gerhard, Gunter, Gunther, Heinz, Hilde.
German and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, a short form of various compound names with the first element bald ‘bold’. See also German Bold , compare Bolt .

variants.name: Bolda
meanings: Polish: from the German personal name Bold (see Bold 4).
Hungarian: from a pet form of the personal name Boldizsár, Hungarian form of Baltazar .

variants.name: Bolt
meanings: English: from Middle English bolt ‘bolt, bar’, also ‘bundle’ (Old English bolt ‘arrow’). One of the most common senses of this word was ‘the blunt-headed bolt of a crossbow’, so it could have been given to an archer or to a maker of iron bars or bolts. Bolt was also used metaphorically for a short, thickset, or tubby person and for someone who was physically upright as a bolt, i.e. ‘as straight as an arrow’.
English: variant of   Bold   1.

Dutch, North German, and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, a short form of various compound names with the first element bald ‘bold’. See also German Bold , compare German and Danish Boldt .

variants.name: Bolton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places in northern England named Bolton (Lancashire, Northumberland, Westmorland, and Yorkshire) or from Boulton in Derbyshire and East Lothian in Scotland, from Old English bothl ‘dwelling, house’ (see Bold 1) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Bolding
meanings: German: patronymic from   Bold   as a personal name.
Danish: habitational name from a place so named in Jutland.
English (Middlesex): variant of   Balding   1.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Bold was born in 1160, in Bold, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William de Bold, was 32 and his mother, Alicia, was 35. He married Waltinia in 1189, in Bold, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1201, in Bold, Lancashire, England, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Bold est né en 1160, à Bold, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Bold, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Alicia, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Waltinia en 1189, à Bold, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1201, à Bold, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Bold 1160 in Bold, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Bold 32 und seine Mutter Alicia 35. Er hat 1189 in Bold, Lancashire, England Waltinia geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1201 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Bold, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Bold è nato il nel 1160 nel Bold, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William de Bold, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Alicia, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Waltinia nel 1189, nel Bold, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1201, nel Bold, Lancashire, England, all'età di 41 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Bold(이)가 1160년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 볼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Bold(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Alicia(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1189년 영국 랭카실르 볼드에서 Waltinia(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1201년 영국 랭카실르 볼드에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Bold nasceu em 1160, em Bold, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William de Bold, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Alicia, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Waltinia em 1189, em Bold, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1201, em Bold, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 41 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Bold родился в 1160 г. в местности болд, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William de Bold было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Alicia, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Waltinia в 1189 г. в местности болд, ланкашир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1201 г. в местности болд, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 41 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Bold nació en 1160, en Bold, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William de Bold, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Alicia, tenía 35 años.
